programmes.show_description: From the origin of polo to the traditions of indigenous tribes, Moeen Latif uncovers the stories of Manipur when he visits Imphal.
Alex meets some veteran sweet shop owners in Kolkata, who teach her to prepare forgotten Bengali sweets.
People flock to Cabo for those perfect beach vacations. Charlie, however, knows a different side to the destination: the action-packed side.
The host visits the iconic sights of Colombo, and goes on to take a river safari and visit a turtle hatchery in Bentota.
From bizarre theme parks to the world's largest man-made marina, Sabrina Bussandri visits Dubai's surreal attractions.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to the Arctic Circle, where she meets Santa Claus in his office. She also tries midnight sun floating.
From fascinating floating villages to the world's only floating national park, Manipur's Loktak lake is home to an intriguing ecosystem.
Called 'khad maas', game meat in Rajasthan used to be slow-cooked in underground pits by soldiers to ensure that enemy troops didn't see any fire.
From dolphin-filled lagoons to colonial charm, Charlie introduces the treasures of the least visited state on the Yucatan peninsula, Campeche.
The host loses herself in the colonial hangover of Galle. She also finds out about unique traditions such as mask making and stilt fishing.
Sabrina Chakici visits Manchester, the city of football and fashion, the city of heritage and hedonism, the city of the industrial revolution.
In Rovaniemi, Emeline Nsingi Nkosi visits a husky farm, tries fishing with a local and participates in a curious Finnish tradition.
Moeen visits some beautiful pagodas in Myanmar, but is in for a shock when on his way back to Manipur, he is stopped by some gun wielding men.
Alex discovers the ingenuity and artistry of Rajasthani cuisine, as she learns to make a traditional meat preparation, which uses meat leftovers.
In the historic city of Merida, Charlie shows around the underground pools, which narrate stories of Mayan beliefs.
The host tries whale watching in Mirissa, goes on a bike tour in Wellawaya and explores tea gardens in Ella..
Jamie Srubis tries a massage inspired by the waves of the sea and a chocolate vanilla body wrap at the Gaya Island Resort in Malaysia.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi bakes cookies with Santa's helpers in Elves village, in Levi. She also rides river rapids on a car inner tube..
Moeen finds out the history of local headhunting and the curious ways of the regional tribes at Ukhrul in Manipur.
After finding his grandparents' handwritten recipes, Vikramaditya Sodawas, the owner of Karni Fort, decided to revive his family's prized recipes.
Secret diving spots, under-the-radar lagoons, ancient Mayan ruins - there is a lot more to the Yucatan peninsula than Cancun and Playa del Carmen.
The host learns about the traditions, beliefs and history of Sri Lanka in the ancient cities of Kandy, Polonnaruwa and Sigiriya.
Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.
Through Finnish activities such as foraging and reindeer herding, Emeline Nsingi Nkosi learns about Sisu - the Finnish secret to happiness.
Moeen finds himself mesmerized by the colourful displays of tribal culture at the famous Hornbill festival of Nagaland.
Alex visits Karni Fort and Fort Begu in Rajasthan, where she unearths some prized dishes of the nobilities.
Tulum, one of the last cities built and inhabited by the Mayans; Charlie narrates the stories about their reign.
Alex visits Taiwan's Penghu island, popular for its tropical weather and sun kissed beaches, bundled with a host of water adventures.
A blend of Bhutan's Dzong architecture and modern design and enhanced with classical hand-drawn Buddhist murals, Taj Tashi lies in the midst of tall mountains and lush landscapes of Thimphu.
Krisann and Nathan's vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.
Originally built as a private palace, Le Palais in Prague is now the perfect place for traditional Czech cuisine and royal Czech hospitality.

The Night is alive without a dull moment. Welcome for a dreamy rendezvous with the bewitching night and stay awake as we roll-out to you five sun-down options to spice your sojourn in the Philippines.
Hungry for stories and flavours of a bygone kingdom, Alex meets Rana Sawai Harisingh II, the 24th descendant to the royal family of Begu.
Charlie introduces the indigenous handicrafts of Oaxaca, including the whimsical creatures with a unique story.
From bizarre theme parks to the world's largest man-made marina, Sabrina Bussandri visits Dubai's surreal attractions.
On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.
Emeline Nsingi Nkosi explores the magnificent monuments and architecturally striking attractions of Helsinki.
After witnessing fascinating regional art forms at the Sangai Festival in Imphal, Moeen tries his hand at Thang Ta, a local martial arts form.
The Khajuraho group of temples have awed generations with their architectural brilliance, intricate carvings and their famous erotic sculptures.
Charlie's journey across Mexico begins in La Paz, a quiet escape sitting on the Sea of Cortez that is often overshadowed by Cabo.
In Kumbakonam, Emeline experiences the rhythms of rural life and the living traditions of Tamil culture.
Many European colonies vied for dominance and cultural supremacy in Bengal. As a result, its cuisine imbibed many Western influences.
